markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 378 **Domain** [Passion](/Domains.aspx?ID=88) **Area** 15-foot emanation **Defense** Will **Duration** 1 minute --- You become intensely entrancing, and creatures are distracted by you as long as they remain within the area. You can exclude any creatures you choose from the effects. If a creature is in the aura when you Cast the Spell or when it enters the aura for the first time, it must attempt a Will saving throw. If a creature leaves and reenters, it uses the results of its original save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 hour.
**Success** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with you for its next action, then is temporarily immune for 1 hour.
**Failure** The creature is fascinated with you.
**Critical Failure** The creature is fascinated with you, and its attitude toward you improves by one step. --- **Heightened (+1)** Increase the size of the emanation by 15 feet.
name: Captivating Adoration

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Perfected Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1830)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=554" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 378

**Domain**
[Perfection](/Domains.aspx?ID=89)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You meditate upon perfection to remove all distractions from your mind. Attempt a new Will save against one [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect currently affecting you that required a Will save. Use the result of this new save to determine the outcome of the mental effect, unless the new save would have a worse result than the original save, in which case nothing happens. You can use _perfected mind_ against a given effect only once.
name: Perfected Mind

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Protector's Sacrifice](/Spells.aspx?ID=1832)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=554" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 378

**Domain**
[Protection](/Domains.aspx?ID=90)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** An ally within 30 feet takes damage

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You protect your ally by suffering in their stead. Reduce the damage the ally would take by 3. You redirect this damage to yourself, but your immunities, weaknesses, resistances and so on do not apply

 You aren't subject to any conditions or other effects of whatever damaged your ally (such as [poison](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=24) from a venomous bite). Your ally is still subject to those effects even if you redirect all of the triggering damage to yourself.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage you redirect increases by 3.
name: Protector's Sacrifice

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Whispering Quiet](/Spells.aspx?ID=1834)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=554" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 379

**Domain**
[Secrecy](/Domains.aspx?ID=91)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Area** 15-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You suppress sound in an area, preventing anyone from giving away valuable secrets. This doesn't prevent those in the area from talking or Casting a Spell, but no creature more than 5 feet away can hear its voice without succeeding at a Perception check against your spell DC, which might interfere with [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) or [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) effects as well as communication. Other sounds created in the area are muffled to a similar volume unless that sound is created by an effect with the [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) trait.
name: Whispering Quiet

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Safeguard Secret](/Spells.aspx?ID=1835)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=554" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 379

**Domain**
[Secrecy](/Domains.aspx?ID=91)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** you and any number of willing allies
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour
</row>

</column>

---

You ensure a secret remains safe from prying spies. Choose one piece of information that at least some of the targets know, such as “the location of the stolen treasure” or “the password to enter the guild house.” The spell grants those who know the piece of knowledge you have chosen a +4 status bonus to skill checks (typically [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks) to conceal this knowledge, to saving throws against spells that specifically attempt to obtain this knowledge from them, and to effects that would force them to reveal it.

 If you Cast this Spell again, any previous _safeguard secret_ you had cast ends.
name: Safeguard Secret

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 379 **Domain** [Sun](/Domains.aspx?ID=92) **Area** 15-foot cone **Defense** Fortitude --- You raise your religious symbol and create a blinding flash of light. Each creature in the area must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round and dazzled for 1 minute. The creature can spend an Interact action rubbing its eyes to end the blinded condition.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 round and dazzled for 1 hour. --- **Heightened (3rd)** The area increases to a 30-foot cone.
name: Dazzling Flash

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Vital Luminance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1837)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=554" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 379

**Domain**
[Sun](/Domains.aspx?ID=92)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Drawing life force into yourself, you become a beacon of vitality. You glow with [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 30-foot emanation (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) to the next 30 feet), and you gain an internal pool of light called a luminance reservoir, which begins with a value of 4. At the start of each of your turns, increase your reservoir by 4.

 If an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature damages you with an attack or spell while it's within the bright light of your aura, that creature takes vitality damage equal to half your luminance reservoir value. It takes this damage only the first time it damages you in a round.

 You can Dismiss this Spell. When you do, you can target a creature within your light and direct the vitality energy into it. The target must be a willing living creature or an undead creature. This heals a living target or deals vitality damage to an undead target equal to your luminance reservoir's value. If you Cast this Spell again, any previous casting ends.

---
**Heightened (+1)** Both the initial value of your luminance reservoir and the amount it increases each turn increase by 1.
name: Vital Luminance

[Agile Feet](/Spells.aspx?ID=1838)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=554" />

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the end of the current turn
</row>

</column>

---

The blessings of your god make your feet faster and your movements more fluid. You gain a +5-foot status bonus to your Speed and ignore [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). As part of casting agile feet, you can Step, Stride, or [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29); you can instead [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) if you have the appropriate Speed.
name: Agile Feet

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 380 **Domain** [Trickery](/Domains.aspx?ID=94) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** 1 minute --- You rarely settle for being in just one place. Choose a location within 100 feet of the target that the target can see. You create an illusion of yourself there that only it can see and that mimics all your actions. The target must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target believes you're in the designated location and can't see you in your actual location. The target automatically disbelieves the illusion when you use an action that doesn't make sense in the illusion's position, or if the target attacks, touches, Seeks, or otherwise engages with the illusion. If you use a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against the target, the spell ends.
**Failure** As success, but the target must succeed at a Will save to disbelieve the illusion when one of the listed events occurs.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the target must critically succeed at a Will save to disbelieve when one of the listed events occurs.
name: Trickster's Twin

[Word of Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=1842)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=554" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 380

**Domain**
[Truth](/Domains.aspx?ID=95)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You speak a statement that you believe to be true and that is free of any attempt to deceive through twisting words, omission, and so on. The statement must be 25 words or fewer. A symbol of your deity glows above your head and lasts for the spell's duration, and anyone who sees you and hears you speak knows instinctively that you believe what you say is true. This assurance of honesty grants you a +2 status bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks as long as the symbol persists. You can Dismiss the spell, and if you say something that you don't believe is entirely true, the spell ends before you complete your statement.
name: Word of Truth

<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=554" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Revelation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=138" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 380

**Domain**
[Truth](/Domains.aspx?ID=95)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 30-foot emanation

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 round
</row>

</column>

---

Divine insight lets you see things as they are, unveiled by attempts to magically cloak the truth. The GM attempts a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against each illusion that is at least partially within the area. Instead of counteracting the illusion, you see through it (for instance, if the check succeeds against an [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) spell, you see the creature's true form but _illusory disguise_ doesn't end). Each time a new illusion comes into the aura during the duration of the spell, the GM attempts a secret counteract check for that illusion.

---
**Heightened (7th)** You can allow everyone within range of the emanation to see through any illusions you succeed against, not just yourself.
name: Glimpse the Truth

The target attempts a Will save. Regardless of the result, it's then temporarily immune for 1 hour. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is stupefied 1 until the end of your next turn.
**Failure** The target is stupefied 2 until the end of your next turn.
**Critical Failure** The target is stupefied 2 for 1 minute and is forced to its knees, becoming [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 381 **Domain** [Wealth](/Domains.aspx?ID=99) **Range** 30 feet **Area** 5-foot burst **Defense** Will **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- You create a brief vision of immense wealth filling the spell's area. Each creature within 20 feet of the area that could be enticed by material wealth must attempt a Will saving throw. A creature that enters the area automatically disbelieves the illusion, and disbelieving the illusion ends any [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) condition imposed by the spell. As long as you Sustain the spell, other creatures react to the treasure like they would any other illusion, but they are not at risk of becoming fascinated. **Critical Success** The creature disbelieves the illusion and is unaffected by it.
**Success** The creature is fascinated by the wealth until it has completed its first action on its next turn.
**Failure** The creature is fascinated by the illusion.
name: Appearance of Wealth

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Precious Metals](/Spells.aspx?ID=1851)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=554" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 381

**Domain**
[Wealth](/Domains.aspx?ID=99)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 metal shield or weapon, 1 suit of metal armor, or up to 1 Bulk of metal material (such as coins or metal-tipped ammunition)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Your deity blesses base metals to transform them into precious materials. The metal in the target transforms from its normal metal into [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272), copper, gold, iron, [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277), or steel. If the spell's rank is 8th or higher, add [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) and [dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=275) to the options. If you transform an object into copper, gold, or silver, its Hardness is reduced to 1. Otherwise, its Hardness is increased to 10 if it was lower. An item transmuted in this way deals damage according to its new material. For example, a steel sword transmuted to cold iron would deal additional damage to a creature with a weakness to cold iron. It can have other effects of the new material at the GM's discretion.

 This change is clearly magical and temporary, so the item's monetary value doesn't change; you couldn't transmute copper coins to gold and use them to purchase something.

---
**Heightened (+1)** If you increase the Hardness of the object, the new Hardness is 2 higher.
name: Precious Metals

<trait label="Cleric" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=554" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Sanctified" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=519" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 381

**Domain**
[Zeal](/Domains.aspx?ID=100)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 weapon you're wielding
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the start of your next turn
</row>

</column>

---

Holding your weapon aloft, you fill it with divine energy. On your next Strike with that weapon before the start of your next turn, you gain a +1 status bonus to the attack roll, the weapon deals an additional 1d6 spirit damage, and the Strike gains the [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519) trait. Weapon surge ends once you complete this Strike or the weapon leaves your possession.

---
**Heightened (5th)** The attack deals 2d6 additional spirit damage.

**Heightened (9th)** The attack deals 3d6 additional spirit damage.
name: Weapon Surge

<title level="1" right="Focus 6" pfs="Standard">
[Primal Summons](/Spells.aspx?ID=1854)
<actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=583" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 381

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You enhance a summoned creature with the power of the elements. If your next action is to cast either [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) or [_summon plant or fungus_](/Spells.aspx?ID=325), choose air, earth, fire, metal, water, or wood; the creature you summon gains the corresponding abilities.

- **Air** The creature gains a fly Speed of 60 feet.
- **Earth** The creature gains a burrow Speed of 20 feet, reduces its land Speed by 10 feet (minimum 5 feet), and gains resistance 5 to physical damage.
- **Fire** The creature's Strikes deal 1d6 extra fire damage, and it gains resistance 10 to fire and weakness 5 to cold and water.
- **Metal** The creature's Strikes deal 1d6 extra electricity damage, and it gains resistance 5 to electricity.
- **Water** The creature gains a swim Speed of 60 feet, can spend 1 action after a melee attack to attempt a [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) (ignoring multiple attack penalty), and gains resistance 5 to fire.
- **Wood** The creature gains a climb Speed of 30 feet and resistance 2 to bludgeoning and piercing damage.
name: Primal Summons

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 382 **Area** ground within a 100-foot emanation **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- The ground within the area transforms into a mass of dangerous briars that assault and impede your foes. When you Cast the Spell and the first time you Sustain it each turn on subsequent rounds, select one of the following effects to occur in the area.
  • **Ensnare** The briars clump around your foes, attempting to hold them in place. A foe within the area (or flying at most 20 feet above the area) must attempt a Reflex save. On a failure, it takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to all Speeds for 1 round, and on a critical failure, it is immobilized for 1 round unless it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79).
  • **Impede** The briars twist and writhe, making the entire area [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453).
  • **Wall** A [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=366) appears in the area, lasting for 1 round. The wall is greater difficult terrain instead of difficult terrain.
In addition, once per round you can direct the briars to impale any target in the area (or flying up to 20 feet above the area) that you can see by using a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) and [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) traits. Make a spell attack roll. On a success, the target takes 10d6 piercing damage and takes a –10- foot circumstance penalty to all Speeds for 1 round; on a critical success, the target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1 round unless it Escapes.
name: Impaling Briars

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 382 **Requirements** You are outdoors and aboveground **Area** 100-foot emanation, which includes you **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- The sky above you darkens in a matter of moments, swirling with ominous clouds punctuated by flashes of lighting. When you Cast the Spell and the first time you Sustain it each turn on subsequent rounds, select one of the following effects to occur in the area.
  • **Calm** No additional effect.
  • **Fog** Heavy fog rolls in, with the effects of [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606).
  • **Rain** Torrential rain falls from the sky, dousing ordinary flames. Creatures in the area take a –2 circumstance penalty to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) and Perception checks.
  • **Wind** Powerful winds buffet the area in all directions. Ranged attacks take a –4 circumstance penalty, and the area is difficult terrain for flying creatures.
In addition, once per round you can use a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) and [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) traits, to call down a bolt of lightning, striking any target in range that you can see. You deal 10d6 electricity damage to the target; it must attempt a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. On a failure, it is also [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 round.
name: Storm Lord

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Untamed Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1861)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=583" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 382

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** varies
</row>

</column>

---

You reach within for a different part of yourself, and you set it free, transforming your body into another form. You polymorph into any form listed in [_pest form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=217), which lasts 10 minutes. All other _untamed form_ shapes last 1 minute. You can add more shapes to your _untamed form_ list with druid feats; your feat might grant  you some or all of the shapes from a given [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) spell. When you transform into a shape granted by a spell, you gain all the effects of the shape you chose from a version of the spell heightened to _untamed form'_s rank. _Untamed form_ allows you to use your own shapeshifting training more easily than most polymorph spells. When you choose to use your own attack modifier while polymorphed instead of the form's default attack modifier, you gain a +2 status bonus to your attack rolls.

---
**Heightened (2nd)** You can transform into shapes listed in [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10).
name: Untamed Form

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 383 **Duration** 1 minute --- You transform just a part of your body. Choose any one effect that matches an untamed order feat you have.
  • **Untamed Form** Your hands transform into incredibly sharp claws. Untamed claws are an unarmed attack, have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) and [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) traits, and deal 1d6 slashing damage. You can still hold and use items with your hands while they're transformed by this spell, but you must have a hand free to attack with it.
  • **Insect Shape** Your mouth becomes deadly jaws. Untamed jaws are an unarmed attack that deal 1d8 piercing damage.
  • **Elemental Shape** You shift your body to be partially composed of elemental matter, granting you resistance 5 to critical hits and precision damage.
  • **Plant Shape** Your arms become long vines, increasing your [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) to 10 feet (or 15 feet with a reach weapon).
  • **Soaring Shape** You must cast _untamed shift_ using 2 actions for this benefit. You grow wings from your back, gaining a fly Speed of 30 feet.
--- **Heightened (6th)** You can choose up to two effects from the list. Untamed claws leave terrible, ragged wounds that also deal 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) on a hit, and untamed jaws are envenomed, also dealing 2d6 persistent poison damage on a hit. **Heightened (10th)** You can choose up to three effects from the list. Untamed claws deal 4d6 persistent bleed damage on a hit, and untamed jaws deal 4d6 persistent poison damage on a hit.
name: Untamed Shift

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 383 **Duration** 1 minute --- Without fully transforming your body, you gain one animalistic feature, which you select from the list below each time you Cast the Spell.
  • **Cat Eyes** You gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416).
  • **Claws** You gain a claw attack that deals 1d6 slashing damage and has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits.
  • **Jaws** You gain a jaws attack that deals 1d8 piercing damage and has the [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) trait.
--- **Heightened (4th)** Add the following options to the list.
  • **Fish Tail** You gain a swim Speed equal to your land Speed.
  • **Owl Eyes** You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).
  • **Wings** You gain a fly Speed equal to your land Speed.
name: Animal Feature

<title level="1" right="Focus 2" pfs="Standard">
[Soothing Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1869)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=682" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 383

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 willing living creature or 1 undead creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You call forth a magical mist that envelops a creature. The mist restores 2d8 Hit Points to a target living creature and ends one source of [persistent acid, bleed, fire, poison, or void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) affecting it. If the creature is taking persistent damage from multiple sources, you select which one is removed. Against an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) target, you deal 2d8 vitality damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save); if it fails the save, it also takes 2 persistent vitality damage.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The amount of healing (or damage to an undead target) increases by 1d8, and the persistent vitality damage to an undead creature increases by 1.
name: Soothing Mist

<title level="1" right="Focus 3" pfs="Standard">
[Ephemeral Tracking](/Spells.aspx?ID=1870)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=682" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 384

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour
</row>

</column>

---

You can look at currents in the air and water and see tracks that you can follow just as you would on land. You can use [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) through both air and water, noticing aerial or aquatic “tracks” of all creatures who passed nearby in the last hour. It's more difficult to Track in this way: the DC to Track through air or water is always at least 30, or higher after precipitation or wind (in the air) or heavy tides or currents (in the water). It's possible to [Cover Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=65) against this spell, but creatures might not realize they need to do so.
name: Ephemeral Tracking

[Ranger's Bramble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1871)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=682" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 384

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 100 feet

**Area** all squares that contain plants in a 5-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You cause plants in the area to entangle your foes, with the effects of [_entangling flora_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515). A creature that critically fails the save takes 2d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) in addition to being [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). Escaping your bramble doesn't end the bleed damage.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The bleed damage on a critical failure increases by 1d4.
name: Ranger's Bramble

<title level="1" right="Focus 5" pfs="Standard">
[Hunter's Vision](/Spells.aspx?ID=1872)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=682" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 384

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 hunted prey
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

Your target glows with a magical aura visible only to you and those who follow your lead. Your target is visible to you and others sharing your Hunt Prey benefits even if it wouldn't normally be due to lighting or the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) or [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) conditions, though cover from opaque objects still blocks your sight. You ignore the flat check against the target due to the concealed condition, and the target isn't automatically hidden from you due to [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409) or being invisible.
name: Hunter's Vision

<title level="1" right="Focus 5" pfs="Standard">
[Terrain Transposition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1873)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=682" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 384

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 90 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You mystically transpose your current location with another. While in a wilderness environment, you transport yourself and items you're holding to a clear space you can see within range. If you have [Favored Terrain](/Feats.aspx?ID=495) and both your starting and ending position are in your favored terrain, the range increases to 180 feet. If you have an animal companion and it is adjacent to you, you can transport it along with you to an adjacent open space, but if this spell would bring any other creature with you, even in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) container, the spell is lost.
name: Terrain Transposition

[Blood Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=1874)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Hex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=324" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=734" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 384

**Lesson**
[Lesson of Protection](/Lessons.aspx?ID=19)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Your patron's aegis descends to shield a target from harm. Designate one of the following creature traits: [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=1), [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20), [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [construct](/Traits.aspx?ID=35), [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50), [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70), [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=77), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=111), [ooze](/Traits.aspx?ID=122), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). The target gains a +1 status bonus to its saving throws and AC against creatures with that trait.

---
**Heightened (5th)** The status bonus increases to +2.
name: Blood Ward

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 384 **Lesson** [Lesson of Death](/Lessons.aspx?ID=24) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- Your patron wraps a hand around your target's heart. The target must attempt a Fortitude saving throw. Regardless of the result, the target is temporarily immune to all _curses of death_ for 1 day. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is afflicted with the curse of death at stage 1, and the stage of the curse can't increase beyond stage 1.
**Failure** The target is afflicted with the curse of death at stage 1.
**Critical Failure** The target is afflicted with the curse of death at stage 2. **Curse of Death** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) This curse ends when the spell ends; **Stage 1** 4d6 void damage and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) (1 round); **Stage 2** 8d6 void damage and fatigued (1 round); **Stage 3** 12d6 void damage and fatigued (1 round); **Stage 4** death --- **Heightened (+1)** Increase the void damage taken on a success and during the first three stages of the curse by 1d6.
name: Curse of Death

<title level="1" right="Focus 3" pfs="Standard">
[Malicious Shadow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1880)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Hex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=324" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=734" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 385

**Lesson**
[Lesson of Shadow](/Lessons.aspx?ID=22)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
AC

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Your patron warps the target's shadow into a deadly form, such as strangling hands, a dangerous weapon, harrying runes, or the like. The shadow moves along with the target, always remaining within reach. When you Cast the Spell, and each time you Sustain it, the shadow Strikes the target. The shadow's Strikes are melee spell attacks that deal 2d10 damage. You choose the type of damage (bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing) when you Cast the Spell. The shadow uses and contributes to your multiple attack penalty. The shadow doesn't take up space, grant flanking, or have any other attributes a creature would. The shadow can't make any attacks other than its Strike.

 The shadow vanishes if the target ceases to cast a shadow (usually if it moves into complete darkness or light). If another effect is controlling the target's shadow when you cast _malicious shadow_, you can attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) that effect to temporarily take control of the shadow for _malicious shadow's_ duration.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The Strike damage increases by 1d10.
name: Malicious Shadow

The target attempts a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes 1d6 cold damage, and the spell ends.
**Failure** The target takes 1d6 cold damage and 1d6 [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The persistent damage automatically ends when the spell ends. It is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to other creatures, and other creatures are concealed to it.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but both the cold damage and the persistent cold damage are 2d6. --- **Heightened (+1)** The cold damage and persistent cold damage increase by 1 (2 on a critical failure).
name: Personal Blizzard

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 386 **Patron Theme** [Silence in Snow](/Patrons.aspx?ID=15) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Reflex **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- Freezing sleet and heavy snowfall collect on the target's feet and legs, dealing 1d4 cold damage and other effects depending on its Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and a –5-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until the spell ends.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage and a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until the spell ends. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d4.
name: Clinging Ice

Your patron deigns to whisper a few secrets. The target can [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84), or [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) as a free action. The target gains a +1 status bonus to the statistic used for the roll (a skill or Perception) on the roll and as long as you Sustain the spell. The target is temporarily immune to discern secrets for 1 minute.

---
**Heightened (5th)** You can target two creatures instead of one.

<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=22" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Hex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=324" />
<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=734" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 387

**Patron Theme**
[Spinner of Threads](/Patrons.aspx?ID=16)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

The barest spin of your patron's spool is enough to alter fate. When the target fails an attack roll, skill check, or saving throw and a +1 status bonus would turn a critical failure into a failure, or failure into a success, you grant the target a +1 status bonus to the check retroactively, changing the outcome appropriately. The spell then ends.

 If you cast _nudge fate_ while a previous casting of this hex is still in effect, the previous effect ends.
name: Nudge Fate

If you cast this hex on a willing ally (for instance, one with [light blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22)), the ally can choose which result it gets without rolling. **Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is shrouded in murky darkness. It treats [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) as [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408), and unless it has [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), all creatures are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to it.
name: Shroud of Night

The target must attempt a Will save; if the creature is an [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=77), or [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), it takes a –1 circumstance penalty to its save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** When the target attempts an attack roll or skill check that would harm you, it takes a –2 status penalty to its roll.
**Failure** As success, but the target also becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) each time it damages you.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the sickened value is 2.
name: Wilding Word

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Area** 5-foot burst or more

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

With a ripple of earth, you raise small barriers from the ground. The ground in the area becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). The spell's area is a 5-foot burst if you spent 1 action to cast it, a 10-foot burst if you spent 2 actions, or a 15-foot burst if you spent 3 actions. A creature can Interact to clear the barriers from one 5-foot square adjacent to it.

---
**Heightened (4th)** You pull the barriers to float in the air, causing the spell to function as difficult terrain for flying creatures.
name: Earthworks

  • You gain a 20-foot status bonus to your Speed.
  • You gain a climb or swim Speed equal to half your Speed.
  • You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).
  • You gain a pair of claws. These are [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170) [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks that deal 1d8 slashing damage.
  • You gain [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) 60 feet ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)).

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Hand of the Apprentice](/Spells.aspx?ID=1906)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=15" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Wizard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=735" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 388

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 500 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
AC

</row>

</column>

---

You take advantage of one of the most fundamental lessons of magic to levitate and propel your weapon. You hurl a held melee weapon with which you are trained at the target, making a spell attack roll. On a success, you deal the weapon's damage as if you had hit with a melee Strike, but add your spellcasting attribute modifier to damage, rather than your Strength modifier. On a critical success, you deal double damage, and you add the weapon's [critical specialization effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=235). Regardless of the outcome, the weapon flies back to you and returns to your hand.
name: Hand of the Apprentice

---

You gather the embers of another caster's spell, using your understanding of unified magic to pick apart their formulas and incantations well enough to imitate the spell yourself, if only for a short time. Until the end of your next turn, you can Cast the triggering Spell by expending a wizard spell slot of the same rank. That caster's spells can't trigger your _interdisciplinary incantation_ again for 24 hours.
name: Interdisciplinary Incantation

**Source** [Wake the Dead #4](/Sources.aspx?ID=219) pg. 29 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283) **Range** 5 feet **Duration** 1 minute --- You create a miniature replica of the infamous Bonewall of Yled, a jumble of bones with threatening limbs reaching from it. The bulwark is 10 feet high, 10 feet long, and 1 foot thick. The bulwark has an AC of 10 and 30 Hit Points. You must create the bulwark in an unbroken open space so it doesn’t pass through any creatures or objects, or the bulwark's dimensions are reduced to fit the smaller space. It must pass within 5 feet of you.
The _bonewall bulwark_ grants cover from creatures on the opposite side. At the beginning of your turn, you may have the limbs attack a single creature you're aware of within 5 feet of the wall using your spell attack modifier and dealing 3d10 bludgeoning damage. This ignores cover and concealment provided by the _bonewall bulwark_. --- **Heightened (+1)** The bludgeoning damage increases by 1d10 and the HP of the wall increases by 10.
name: Bonewall Bulwark

Living creatures in the area must attempt a Will save. This gains the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits for living creatures. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.

Creatures in the space where you create the cage can attempt a Reflex save; if they fail or dont attempt the save, they're trapped within it. The spell automatically fails if the space contains a creature larger than the spell's area.
A creature inside the cage is immobilized until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79), uses an effect that can pass through solid objects, or destroys the cage. The cage has AC 10, Hardness 5, and 10 Hit Points, and it's immune to critical hits and precisions damage.
The _osseus cage_ provides lesser cover from attacks that pass through the cage, either from the outside to the inside, or vice versa --- **Heightened (+2)** The spell's area grows by 5 feet on aside and it's Hit Points increase by 20.
name: Osseous Cage

May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 71 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** touch **Target** 1 creature **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- An understanding of how violence begets more violence fills your target, causing it mental anguish when it attempts to take violent actions. The target must attempt a Will saving throw. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The next time the target takes a hostile action, the target takes 1d4 mental damage; the spell's duration then ends.
**Failure** The first time in a round when the target takes a hostile action, the target takes 1d4 mental damage.
**Critical Failure** Each time the target takes a hostile action, the target takes 1d4 mental damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** The mental damage increases by 1d4.
name: Cycle of Retribution

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 71 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 living creature **Duration** 1 minute --- You draw upon the target's negative fate to afflict it and its nearby allies with the unintended consequences of its past lives. Spiritual echoes of the target's past lives instill overwhelming feelings of doubt, regret, and despair into the minds of the target and its nearby allies. The effects depend on the target's Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 4d6 mental damage, and then an aura of shimmering mist with a 10-foot emanation surrounds the creature. This mist isn't dense enough to affect sight, but it does cause the creature and any of its allies in the aura to take a –1 status penalty to all skill checks.
**Failure** As success, but the creature takes an initial 8d6 mental damage and is then [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for the duration of the effect.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the creature takes an initial 16d6 mental damage, then is slowed 1 for the duration of the effect. Any of the target's allies who begin their turn in the 10-foot emanation is slowed 1 for 1 round.
name: Fateful Condemnation

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Limited">
[Fated Healing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1921)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=101" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 71

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 2 creature who are hostile toward each other
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 5 rounds
</row>

</column>

---

You speak about the consequences of actions people take against each other and how it's possible to break cycles of violence simply by making a different choice. The targets regain 1d4 Hit Points at the end of their own turns while the spell is in effect. If a target uses a hostile action against the other target, the spell ends for the target that used the hostile action.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The targets regain an additional 1d4 Hit Points at the end of their own turns.
name: Fated Healing

May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 75 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 2 to 4 creatures in range **Saving Throw** Will **Duration** 3 rounds --- You cause a swirl of energy to entangle the fates of all caught within the blast. Each creature in the area must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature's fate becomes distorted, and momentary conflicting glimpses of the immediate future cause the creature to become [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of its next turn.
**Failure** As success, but the creature is flat-footed for the duration of the spell. If more than one of the targets failed to resist this spell, the creature also becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for the duration of the spell as its fate and those of the other creatures continue to clash and strain against each other.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but any creature that becomes stupefied 1 for the duration of the spell also becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 and [clumsy ](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61)1 for the spell's duration.
name: Entangle Fate

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="">
[Life's Fresh Bloom](/Spells.aspx?ID=1923)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 75

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 20-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 5 rounds
</row>

</column>

---

The ground around you blooms with life, sprouting sparkling grass, flowers, and small shrubs. Each living and non-nindoru creature that starts its turn standing on the ground in the area recovers 1d6 Hit Points and gains a +5-foot status bonus to its Speed until the end of its next turn. Nindoru fiends and undead that start their turn in the area of life's fresh bloom instead become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 unless they succeed at a Fortitude saving throw.

---
**Heightened (7th)** Increase the Hit Points restored to 1d8 and the sickened condition value to 2.

**Heightened (10th)** Increase the Hit Points restored to 1d10 and the sickened condition value to 3.
name: Life's Fresh Bloom

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="">
[Prophet's Luck](/Spells.aspx?ID=1924)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Misfortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=110" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 76

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

**Trigger** You or a creature within range attempts a saving throw against an obvious threat.

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You prophesize the result of the triggering saving throw as either a success (including a critical success) or a failure (including a critical failure). The target gains a +1 status bonus to the save if you predict success or a –1 status penalty if you predict failure. If you predict the result correctly, fortune favors you, and you gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws. If you predict incorrectly, fortune spurns the target, imparting a –1 status penalty to its saving throws.

---
**Heightened (6th)** The save bonus or penalty increases to +2 or –2.

**Heightened (9th)** The save bonus or penalty increases to +3 or –3.
name: Prophet's Luck

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="">
[Sacred Nimbus](/Spells.aspx?ID=1925)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 76

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 10-foot square

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
Reflex

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You summon a small, fluffy white nimbus cloud that floats 10 feet above the ground and rains down pleasantly warm sanctified water. The rain deals 5d6 good damage to fiends, undead, and creatures that have a weakness to good damage in the area at the end of your turn. When you Sustain the Spell, you can move the cloud at a fly speed of 15 feet to anywhere in the spell's range. The water dissipates as it makes contact with any creatures or surfaces, leaving only a bit of humidity in its place.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increaes by 1d6
name: Sacred Nimbus

Any nindoru fiend or undead that begins its turn adjacent to you must attempt a Will save with the following results. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and immune to this spell's effects for the remainder of the spell's duration.

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="">
[Wall of Mirrors](/Spells.aspx?ID=1927)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 76

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You create a solid wall of shimmering reflective glass up to 50 feet long and 20 feet high. The wall is an inch thick and stands vertically. You must create the wall in an unbroken open space so its edges don't pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost. Each 10-foot-by-10-foot section of the wall has AC 10, Hardness 10, and 40 Hit Points, and it's immune to critical hits and precision damage. Everything on each side of the wall is concealed from creatures on the opposite side. If a creature Strikes the wall, the wall causes the creature to endure the mental anguish of that attack's pain as well; the creature takes 4d4 mental damage and can resist this damage with a basic Will save.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The Hit Points of each section of the wall increase by 10.
name: Wall of Mirrors

<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

---

Your nose becomes more sensitive to the scents of the wild, the better for you to track your quarry. For the spell’s duration, you gain [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=2412) as an [imprecise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) with a range of 30 feet. Your [hunted prey](/Actions.aspx?ID=2257) can’t be [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) by you as long as it’s within your scent range.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** Your scent range increases to 60 feet.
name: Keen Smell

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 59 **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Reflex --- You spit toxic goo that coats your target’s face and eyes, dealing 2d6 poison damage. It must attempt a Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target takes no damage.
**Success** The target takes half damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round, though it can Interact to wipe its eyes and remove the condition.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and is dazzled until the end of your next turn.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage, is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round, and is dazzled until it uses an Interact action to wipe its eyes. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d6.
name: Slime Spit

[Canopy Crawler](/Spells.aspx?ID=1931)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=657" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=682" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** yourself or your animal companion
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

The target grows a prehensile tail, or gains prehensile qualities in their existing tail, to help them climb with ease. For the duration of the spell, they gain a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) equal to their highest Speed. The target can [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) with a hand occupied (or two hands occupied if they have the [Combat Climber](/Feats.aspx?ID=5134) feat).

---
**Heightened (5th)** The spell’s duration increases to 1 hour, and you can target both yourself and your animal companion with a single casting.
name: Canopy Crawler

<title level="1" right="Focus 2" pfs="Standard">
[Imitate Fauna](/Spells.aspx?ID=1932)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=682" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 59

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 willing  animal 
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour
</row>

</column>

---

You use your knowledge of nature to pass the target off as a common animal of the same size, representing a species chosen when the spell is cast. This covers the target’s visual appearance, scent, and voice. The target can communicate with other members of the species as though they shared a language but can’t speak other languages while the spell persists. Casting _imitate fauna_ counts as setting up a disguise for the purpose of the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) action. It allows the target to ignore any circumstance penalties they might take for being disguised as dissimilar creatures, and it gives the target a +4 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to prevent other animals from seeing through their disguise. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this spell.

---
**Heightened (5th)** You can target up to six willing animals.
name: Imitate Fauna

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 60 **Area** 10-foot emanation **Defense** Will --- You appear larger and stronger to nearby creatures, appearing to possess threatening features like antlers, claws, or poison to their senses. Each non-allied creature in the area must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1. This condition doesn’t decrease at the end of its turn if it damaged you during that turn.
**Failure** As success, but frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** As success, but frightened 3.
name: Threatening Mimicry

---

Your skin becomes painted with vivid stripes or bright aposematic swirls, warning other creatures of its toxic qualities. Any creature that touches you or hits you with an unarmed melee attack takes 2d8 poison damage. If you have an animal companion, you can cast this spell on them instead, with a range of 30 feet.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The poison damage increases by 1d8.
name: Warning Stripes

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 60 **Range** 120 feet **Area** all squares that contain plants in a 20-foot burst **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- Plants in the area grow rapidly, taking on carnivorous characteristics as they seek to consume prey. All affected squares are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366), both on the ground and for flying creatures. Each creature that begins its turn in the area must attempt a Reflex save unless it's already [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90). **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is grabbed until the beginning of its next turn or it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296).
**Failure** The creature is grabbed until the spell ends or it Escapes.
**Critical Failure** The creature is restrained until the spell ends or it Escapes. When you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this spell, each creature grabbed or restrained by the plants takes 4d6 piercing damage . --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage dealt by the plants when you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell increases by 2d6.
name: Gluttonous Growth

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 60 **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** 1 minute --- You can deceive creatures by changing their perception of their allies’ behavior. Each affected creature suspects their allies have changed allegiance, depending on the outcome of their Will save. Regardless of the effects of the saving throw, the creature is then temporarily immune to _pack breaker_ for 1 hour. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=97) to all creatures to which it wasn’t already [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80), even those that were previously allies. It treats no one as an ally for 1 minute. Each of its former allies within its reach must attempt a save against _pack breaker_.
**Failure** As success, but the creature is also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute.
name: Pack Breaker

[Pulverizing Wake](/Spells.aspx?ID=1937)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=540" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=682" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=697" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 60

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 15-foot cone

**Target** 1 creature within reach
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
AC and [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude (see text)

</row>

</column>

---

Imitating raptorial creatures, you attack with such speed it drives a sonic shockwave. Make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against a creature in your reach; if you hit, you deal an additional 3d8 sonic damage. Regardless of the result of your Strike, that creature becomes the point of origin of a 15-foot cone, aimed directly away from you. All creatures in that cone take 6d8 sonic damage (basic Fortitude save).

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage of the cone increases by 1d8.
name: Pulverizing Wake

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Scrounger's Glee](/Spells.aspx?ID=1938)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=598" />
<trait label="Hex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=624" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=734" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 61

**Patron Theme**
[Devourer of Decay](/Patrons.aspx?ID=19)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Will

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

With a cruel laugh and a flash of your canines, you make a target understand that death is coming to claim it and that its demise will serve to strengthen another. The target becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) if it fails a Will save (or frightened 2 on a critical failure). This condition can't be reduced below 1 while the spell is active and the target can hear you. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell as a reaction when an ally critically succeeds at a Strike against the target, restoring 1d4 Hit Points to that ally.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The number of Hit Points restored when you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell increases by 1d4.
name: Scrounger's Glee

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 61 **Patron Theme** [Ripple in the Deep](/Patrons.aspx?ID=20) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- A long tentacle like that belonging to an octopus, anemone, or man-of-war, dripping with briny water, appears from a glowing blue portal to wrap around the target’s face, blocking its vision. For the duration of the spell, the target must attempt a Fortitude save at the beginning of its turn. The portal follows the target around, and the tentacle can’t be targeted or harmed. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes a –2 penalty to Perception checks involving vision until the end of its turn.
**Failure** The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of its turn.
**Critical Failure** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its turn.
name: Sting of the Sea

<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />

<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=737" />
<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=734" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 61

**Lesson**
[Lesson of the Shark](/Lessons.aspx?ID=28)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Requirements** The target is taking [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) or your last action dealt slashing damage to the target.

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature that can bleed
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Predators are drawn to the scent of blood, and blood can disperse shockingly far in the water. You summon the spirits of aquatic predators to appear as a spectral swarm around the target. When you [Cast](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) or [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this Spell, the target takes 2d6 spirit damage (basic Reflex save). If the target takes any damage from the spell, it treats all water as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for 1 round as the predators try to drag them down. The spectral predators don’t take up space, grant [flanking](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375), or have any other attributes a creature would.

If you deal slashing damage to the target while the spell is active, you automatically Sustain this Spell. If you cast _blood in the water_ while a previous casting of this hex is still in effect, the previous effect ends.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage dealt increases by 2d6.
name: Blood in the Water
requirement_markdown: The target is taking [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) or your last action dealt slashing damage to the target.

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 61 **Lesson** [Lesson of Decay](/Lessons.aspx?ID=26) **Range** touch **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- You infect your target with fungal spores to hasten the cycle of decay. The target must attempt a Fortitude saving throw. [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) that aren’t [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632), such as [skeletons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=472) and [zombies](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=487), don’t have their normal immunity to this disease, as the spores can still colonize their remaining flesh and bone. This might also apply to other non-living creatures made of organic material at the GM’s discretion. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target sprouts mushrooms and is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is afflicted with voidcap spores at stage 1.
**Critical Failure** The target is afflicted with voidcap spores at stage 2. **Voidcap Spores** ([disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) This disease ends when the spell ends
**Stage 1** [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 (1 round)
**Stage 2** enfeebled 2 and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (1 round)
**Stage 3** enfeebled 2, slowed 1, and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 (1 round)
name: Mycological Malady

<title level="1" right="Focus 3" pfs="Standard">
[Sheltering Wings](/Spells.aspx?ID=1943)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Hex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=624" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Witch" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=734" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 61

**Lesson**
[Lesson of the Flock](/Lessons.aspx?ID=27)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** An ally moves through a square within 15 feet of you or uses an action that has the move trait.

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 15 feet

**Target** 1 ally
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Like migratory birds, you know everyone flies farther together. You cause immense wings to sprout from your familiar. Your familiar [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to 30 feet, though it must move toward the triggering ally. If your familiar ends this movement adjacent to the triggering ally, it lands on their back; until the beginning of your next turn, the familiar automatically is carried by the ally, and its wings provide them [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372). When the spell ends, the familiar hops off your ally into an adjacent square.
name: Sheltering Wings

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Songbird's Call](/Spells.aspx?ID=1944)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=542" />
<trait label="Bard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=546" />
<trait label="Composition" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=559" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 65

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 15-foot emanation centered on you

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Your voice calls a storm of songbirds that whirl around you and peck at your enemies. All creatures within the area of whirling birds become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and all creatures outside the area become concealed to creatures within it. A creature other than you that enters or ends its turn in the area takes 1d6 piercing damage as the birds peck at it.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Songbird's Call

<title level="1" right="Focus 2" pfs="Standard">
[Spirit of the Beast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1945)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=606" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=657" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=676" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 67

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** your animal companion
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You bring out the spirit of another animal that lives within your companion. Its body morphs slightly to take on an aspect of the animal you choose to have it emulate—for example, it might grow a pair of horns if the spirit is a bull or a tail if the spirit is a monkey. When you Cast this Spell, select from one of the following aspects.

- **Aspect of Might** Your companion gains a +1 status bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395), and Fortitude saves.
- **Aspect of Swiftness** Your companion gains a +1 status bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), and Reflex saves.
- **Aspect of Insight** Your companion gains a +1 status bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301), [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) or [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408), and Will saves.

---
**Heightened (5th)** The status bonuses increase to +2.

**Heightened (8th)** The status bonuses increase to +3.
name: Spirit of the Beast

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 84 **Traditions** [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** 1 minute (see text) --- You create a spectral albatross, a guiding bird for sailors, to hover around the target. You and allies within 30 feet of the target gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attacks against the target. The target creature can spend an action to Strike the albatross, which automatically succeeds and kills it. The target must then attempt a Will save against your spell DC. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The guilt of slaughtering a bird of good fortune weighs on the target’s mind. The target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The albatross hangs around a cord from the target’s neck (or closest equivalent) for 1 minute, cursing them for their transgression. During this time, the target must roll twice and take the worse result on their next Will save, after which the albatross disappears.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the duration is 1 hour.
name: Albatross Curse
