**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 84 **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 120 feet **Area** 15-foot-radius burst **Defense** Reflex **Duration** 1 minute --- You transmute the ground into a conical pit trap of loose sand that becomes difficult terrain for the duration. A creature or unsecured object that enters the sand or starts its turn in the sand is moved toward the center, depending on the result of its Reflex save. This is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=2364). If there isn’t enough space near the center of the pit, affected creatures and objects move as far as they can without being blocked, up to the amount set by their saving throw outcomes. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature moves 5 feet toward the center.
**Failure** The creature moves 10 feet toward the center.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the creature becomes [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) in the pit. It can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) against your spell DC. --- **Heightened (+2)** Increase the area of the spell and the amount a creature moves on a failure by 5 feet.
name: Antlion Trap

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Camel Spit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1948)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=524" />
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=540" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=657" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 84

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
AC

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You alter your stomach, esophagus, and tongue to be able to spit partially digested food with force. You can spit at a foe once you finish Casting the Spell and can repeat the attack once on each of your subsequent turns by taking a single action, which has the [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), and [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) traits. After your third spit attack, the spell ends.

When you attack with camel spit, make a ranged spell attack roll against a creature within 15 feet, dealing 1d6 acid damage and causing the target to be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round if you hit. On a critical hit, you deal double damage and the target takes 1 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6, and the persistent damage on a critical hit is increased by 1.
name: Camel Spit

[Claws of the Otter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1949)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=555" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 84

<row gap="medium">

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour

---

Webbing grows between your fingers and your nails extend into vicious claws. For the spell's duration, you gain a +1 status bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) and you gain a claws unarmed attack. They're an [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attack that deals 1d4 slashing damage and an additional 1d6 cold damage.

---
**Heightened (+3)** The additional cold damage increases by 1d6.
name: Claws of the Otter

The target must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target's voice becomes hoarse, and speaking becomes painful. Whenever it uses an action that has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait or attempts to Cast a Spell that doesn't have the [subtle](/Traits.aspx?ID=522) trait, it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the action is lost. Once per round, the target can spend an Interact action to massage its throat, attempting a Fortitude save against your spell DC. On a success, the spell ends.
**Failure** As success, but using an action with the auditory trait also deals 2d10 mental damage to the target as the sound of its distorted voice grates on its ears.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the damage for using an action with the auditory trait is doubled, and the target can't use an Interact action to attempt a Fortitude save to end the effect early. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage for using an action with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait increases by 1d10.
name: Croak Voice

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Foraging Friends](/Spells.aspx?ID=1952)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 85

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 10 minutes

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

Giving a cheerful whistle, you call forth a handful of small animals, such as birds or mice, to collect food for you and your allies. The animals return 1 hour later with enough foraged goods to feed four Medium creatures for 1 day, then return to their normal behavior. If you’re in a particularly strange environment, as determined by your GM, you might need a minimum proficiency with primal spell DCs, equivalent to the minimum proficiency required to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) in strange environments.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** The animals bring back enough food for eight Medium creatures for 1 day.

**Heightened (5th)** The animals bring back enough food for 30 Medium creatures for 1 day.
name: Foraging Friends

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Frog Tongue](/Spells.aspx?ID=1953)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 85

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Your tongue extends unnaturally, flicking out toward a creature within range and dealing 2d8 bludgeoning damage (basic Reflex save). On a failure, the creature is also stuck to the end of the tongue. It is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) and can't move beyond the reach of your tongue. A creature can sever the tongue with a Strike that deals at least 10 slashing damage or attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) against your spell DC. The AC of the tongue is equal to your spell DC. Severing the tongue in this way deals no damage to you but ends the spell. While a creature is stuck to the end of your tongue, actions you take with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait take a –2 circumstance penalty. If you move so that the affected creature is outside of the tongue's reach, the spell ends.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d8.
name: Frog Tongue

[Hidebound](/Spells.aspx?ID=1954)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 85

**Deity**
[Iapholi](/Deities.aspx?ID=703)

</row>

---

---
**Heightened (+2)** The resistance increases by 3.

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Hippocampus Retreat](/Spells.aspx?ID=1955)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=540" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=657" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 86

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Requirements** You’re mostly or totally submerged in water
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 10 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
AC

</row>

</column>

---

You temporarily shape your lower limbs into the tail of a hippocampus in order to swim away from a nearby foe after dealing a parting blow. Attempt a melee spell attack roll against the target’s AC, dealing 2d6 bludgeoning damage on a hit (or double damage on a critical hit). Then, [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) up to 30 feet; if you already have a [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351), you can Swim up to your Speed with a +10-foot circumstance bonus. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your AC against reactions triggered by this movement. At the end of the movement, your lower limbs return to normal.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Hippocampus Retreat
requirement_markdown: You’re mostly or totally submerged in water

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 86 **Traditions** [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Area** 60-foot emanation **Defense** Will **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- You emit a plaintive cry to lure your enemies closer. Each creature in the emanation that can hear you must attempt a Will save. If you speak a creature's name as part of the casting of the spell, that creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to its saving throw. Each creature that enters the area on its turn must attempt a save. If you attack or take a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), the [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) condition ends only for the creature that's attacked. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature becomes fascinated and compelled to move toward the sound of your cry on its turn. As long as it is in the emanation and can hear, it must spend at least one of its actions on each of its turns to move closer to you.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature must spend all its actions moving toward the sound. Additionally, the creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58).
name: Luring Wail

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Restricted">
[Primal Chorus](/Spells.aspx?ID=1957)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 86

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 1-mile burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until your next daily preparations
</row>

</column>

---

You let loose a primal howl that incites animals and beasts to join in the chorus. Doing so gives you a general idea of how many creatures with the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) or [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=547) trait are within the range of the spell, but significant creatures can attempt a Will save against your spell DC to resist responding to your call. You gain a +1 status bonus to your next Initiative roll in an encounter with an enemy creature that replies to your call. For purposes of using [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) during [exploration mode](/Rules.aspx?ID=2440), you can communicate with a creature that responds to your call for the duration of the spell. You can only make simple commands, such as approach or hide, and only understand simple ideas, such as compliance with the order or the presence of natural hazards.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The status bonus increases by 1.
name: Primal Chorus

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Sacred Beasts](/Spells.aspx?ID=1958)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 86

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Requirements** You worship a deity
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 5-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

</row>

</column>

---

You call out to the creatures of the wild favored by your deity. You quickly summon your deity’s sacred animal (or a small swarm of them if the animal is usually Tiny). For example, you would call forth a [lion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2866) if you worship [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=285) or a swarm of [spiders](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=474) if you worship [Norgorber](/Deities.aspx?ID=289). If your deity doesn’t have a known sacred animal, work with the GM to find a thematic one. The animal or swarm assaults all creatures in the area, dealing 2d6 damage. The damage is either bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing based on the animal that was conjured, as determined by the GM. After their attacks, the animals return to your deity’s plane.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d6.
name: Sacred Beasts

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Snake Fangs](/Spells.aspx?ID=1959)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=657" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 86

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Your jaw unhinges as your teeth extend into wicked fangs. For the spell's duration, you gain a fangs unarmed attack. They're a [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attack that deals 1d8 piercing damage and an extra 2d10 poison damage. If you have a creature at least one size smaller than you [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) with your fangs, you can use the [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) ability that deals 4d6 bludgeoning damage and has a Rupture value of 17. A swallowed creature is transported to an extraplanar space that resembles the inside of a snake's stomach, so when it gets free, it appears in a space adjacent to you. If you're killed or the spell ends, the swallowed creature is immediately freed.

---
**Heightened (+3)** The extra poison damage of your fangs unarmed attack increases by 1d10, the damage dealt by the Swallow Whole ability increases by 6d6, and the Rupture value increases by 9.
name: Snake Fangs

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 87 **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 120 feet **Defense** Will and [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex (see text) **Duration** until the end of your next turn --- You summon an unstoppable stampede of panicking beasts. Whether you conjure wild beasts or tamed cattle, the stampede is a force of nature that leaves behind nothing in its wake. The stampede occupies the space of a Gargantuan creature and has a Speed of 60 feet. **Arrive** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) _Foreboding Tremors_ The sheer energy of a stampede can cause even apex predators to panic. Each creature within a 60-foot emanation must attempt a Will save with the following effects.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3 and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for 1 round. **Depart** _Flatten the Earth_ The stampede Strides up to double its Speed, trampling each Large or smaller creature, hazard, and structure whose space it enters, dealing 8d8 bludgeoning damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The stampede ignores and attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) all [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) it enters caused by debris, overgrowth, rubble, or thick ground cover.
name: Summon Stampede

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 87 **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 100 feet **Defense** see text **Duration** until the end of your next turn --- You briefly call forth the spirit of one of the [Wardens of the Wild](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=506), the legendary guardians of nature. The warden occupies the space of a [Gargantuan](/Rules.aspx?ID=2359) creature. When you [Cast this Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), you summon a warden depending on the biome in which the spell is cast (for instance, summoning the Warden of Caverns and Burrows if summoned in a cave, canyon, or other underground environment). In an unnatural environment, the warden summoned depends on the region's most recent or similar natural biome, as decided by the GM. - **[Warden of Caverns and Burrows](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3325)** Speed 60 feet, burrow 100 feet.
**Arrive** ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) _Obsidian Cage_ Obsidian stalagmites burst from the earth as the warden surfaces, impaling enemies and trapping them in stalagmites. Enemies on the ground within 40 feet take 6d6 piercing damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures that fail their save become [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until they [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (the DC is your spell DC).
**Depart** ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) _Blessing of the Depths_ The squares occupied by the warden remain disheveled and broken, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). The warden affects you and all your allies within 60 feet with a [_mountain resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610) and [_unfettered pack_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1733). The duration for both spells is reduced to 3 rounds, but the duration of _mountain resilience_ isn't reduced when an affected target is hit by a bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing attack. - **[Warden of Forests and Meadows](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3326)** Speed 120 feet.
**Arrive** ([illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) _Idyllic Panorama_ The warden's presence unpredictably warps the appearances and perceived distances of creatures and obstacles around it. Each time an enemy within 40 feet of the warden attempts a Strike or Stride action, it takes 4d6 mental damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). On a critical failure, the creature Strikes a random target within reach or Strides in a random direction. On a critical success, the creature becomes temporarily immune for 1 round.
**Depart** _Fox's Insightful Trickery_ The warden affects you and all your allies within 60 feet with [_blur_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455) and [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727). The duration for both spells is reduced to 3 rounds. - **[Warden of Oceans and Rivers ](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3327)** Speed 60 feet, swim 120 feet.
**Arrive** _I Am the Tide_ If the warden is summoned in a body of water, it creates a churning whirlpool that deals 10d6 bludgeoning damage to all creatures within 30 feet ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature that fails this save is [pulled](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 15 feet closer to the warden (30 feet on a critical failure). If the warden is summoned outside of a body of water, it creates a wave of water that deals 8d6 bludgeoning damage to all creatures within 30 feet ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature that fails this save is pushed 10 feet away from the warden.
**Depart** _Acclimation to the Deep_ The warden affects you and your allies within 60 feet with [_feet to fins_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1525) and [_water breathing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1755). The duration for both spells is reduced to 3 rounds, but a creature affected by _feet to fins_ retains its normal land Speed. - **[Warden of Peaks and Skies](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3328)** Speed 60 feet, fly 240 feet.
**Arrive** _Precipice Plummet_ The warden appears, flying downward from the sky and sends a shockwave where she lands. Creatures within 20 feet take 10d6 sonic damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). Creatures that critically fail their saves are [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Depart** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)) _Song of the Skies_ The warden affects you and all your allies within 60 feet with [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) and [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732). The duration for both spells is reduced to 3 rounds.
name: Summon Warden of the Wild

<title level="1" right="Focus 3" pfs="Standard">
[Beastmaster Trance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1962)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 189

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 1 mile

**Target** one animal companion you can perceive
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You enter a trance that allows you to see through the senses of the target companion. You attempt Perception checks using your own Perception, but you share special senses the target has, such as [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416). This spell grants no special method of communication with the chosen animal.

 For the duration of the trance, your own senses are muted. This lack of awareness makes you [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). You can't take actions with the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540) or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) traits.

---
**Heightened (6th)** Increase the range to 100 miles and the duration of sustained up to 10 minutes. The target can hear you through the spell, allowing you to [Command the Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) or use other actions that have the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait.

**Heightened (8th)** Increase the range to planetary and the duration of sustained up to 1 hour. You can communicate telepathically with the target for the duration of the trance.
name: Beastmaster Trance

If the target wishes to prevent you from doing so, it can attempt a Will save, negating the spell on a success, but most [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) don't bother to do so. While tapping into the target's senses, you can't use your own body's senses, but you can change back and forth from your body's senses to the target's senses using a [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) action.

[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex

---

You use your mind to manipulate unattended objects in the area, temporarily animating them to attack. The objects hover in the air, then hurl themselves at nearby creatures in a chaotic flurry of debris. This assault deals 2d10 bludgeoning damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) to each creature in the area. On subsequent rounds, the first time each round you Sustain this spell, it deals 1d10 bludgeoning damage (basic Reflex save) to each creature in the area.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The initial damage increases by 2d10, and the subsequent damage increases by 1d10.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 240 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deities** [Haagenti](/Deities.aspx?ID=430), [Kugaptee](/Deities.aspx?ID=725), [Yamasoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=587), [Zeaki](/Deities.aspx?ID=707) **Range** touch **Target** 1 living humanoid **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** varies --- You tap into the target's inner being and curse it to become a bestial version of itself. The effect is based on its Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target's body gains minor bestial features. Its insides churn as they partially transform, causing it to be [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) for 1 round. When it recovers from the clumsy condition, its features revert to normal, and the spell ends.
**Failure** The target transforms into a bestial form for 1 hour. The target becomes clumsy 1 and gains weakness 1 to [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379). It gains a claw, hoof, horn, or jaws Strike (your choice) that uses the target's unarmed Strike statistics except that the damage type changes to bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing, as appropriate. Whenever the target attempts to use any [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) action, it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the action is lost.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the duration is unlimited.
name: Bestial Curse

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 241 **Traditions** [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deity** [The Lady of the North Star](/Deities.aspx?ID=303) **Defense** Will **Duration** 10 minutes --- A flowing cloak of utter darkness drapes over you, filled with pinpricks of light like distant stars. It imparts the stillness of the cosmos to you, granting you a +2 status bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). While outside under a starry night sky, you're also [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) as long as you remain still. When moving under a starry night sky, you're [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) instead. Gazing too closely into the stars is disorienting. Any creature that ends its turn adjacent to you must attempt a Will save; this is a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effect. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** The creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) and dazzled until the end of its next turn.
name: Blanket of Stars

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 241 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Bloodline** [Aesir](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=29) **Trigger** A creature damages you. **Range** 60 feet **Target** the triggering creature **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- You curse the target with your outrage at being attacked. The effect is determined by the target's Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target can't [observe](/Conditions.aspx?ID=84) you until the end of its turn, and if you're currently observed by it, you become [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) to it.
**Failure** As success, and for 1 minute, every time the target damages you, it can't observe you until the end of its turn.
**Critical Failure** As success, and for an unlimited duration, the first time each round the target damages a creature, it can't observe that creature until the end of its turn. If it damages several creatures at once, the creature it can't perceive is chosen randomly among those creatures.
name: Blinding Fury

The target must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target grows one blister. You can spend a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait, to pop a blister. The target and each creature in a 15-foot cone originating from the target takes 7d6 acid damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). You choose the direction of the cone, which can't include the target. When no blisters are left, the spell ends.
**Failure** As success, but the target grows two blisters.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the target grows four blisters. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage of a popped blister increases by 1d6.

Your words deal 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and the target must attempt a Will save. <title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Bullhorn](/Spells.aspx?ID=1971)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=550" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 241

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You amplify your voice, loud enough for you to be heard easily at a great distance. For the duration, your voice can be heard loudly and clearly by all listeners within 500 feet, even if other ambient noise would otherwise block the sound. Despite the volume, this doesn't make your voice jarring or distracting. This doesn't increase the range or area of other [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) effects, and physical barriers such as walls and doors still block or muffle your voice as normal.

 Your loud voice makes it easier to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) others, and the acoustics assist in [Performing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401) at a large venue. You gain a +1 status bonus to checks to Coerce and auditory [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks to Perform at a large venue.

 You can Dismiss the spell.

---
**Heightened (5th)** Your voice can be heard clearly up to 1,200 feet away.

**Heightened (7th)** Your voice can be heard clearly up to 1 mile away
name: Bullhorn

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Carryall](/Spells.aspx?ID=1972)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=610" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 242

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deities**
[Casandalee](/Deities.aspx?ID=335), [Horus](/Deities.aspx?ID=516)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 8 hours
</row>

</column>

---

A small platform of magical force materializes adjacent to you to carry cargo. It is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) or has a ghostly appearance, is 2 feet in diameter, and follows 5 feet behind you, floating just above the ground. It holds up to 5 Bulk of objects (if they can fit on it). Any objects atop the platform fall to the ground when the spell ends. You can Sustain the spell to move the platform up to 30 feet along the ground, to make it stay in place, or to have it return to you and resume following you.

 The spell ends if a creature tries to ride atop the platform, if the platform is overloaded, if anyone tries to lift or force the platform higher above the ground, or if you move more than 60 feet away from the platform.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The platform can carry 10 Bulk, creatures can ride atop it, and it can hover in the air, not just on the ground.
name: Carryall

[Chameleon Coat](/Spells.aspx?ID=1973)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 242

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Deity**
[Drokalion](/Deities.aspx?ID=702)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 15 feet

**Target** up to 8 creatures
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You shift the colors of the targets' outermost layer of clothing and gear to be closer to their environment when they remain still. Creatures affected by the spell gain a +3 status bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404). The changed color granted by the spell always shifts to match the environment, even if there are drastic changes. If any piece of gear or clothing affected by the spell is removed from a creature, the spell ends for that creature.

---
**Heightened (6th)** If a creature affected by this spell rolls a critical failure on its Stealth check to [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) within 30 feet of a creature that would spot it, it instead only fails its check, as the spell mildly hypnotizes the spotter.

**Heightened (8th)** As 6th rank, and the status bonus is +4.
name: Chameleon Coat

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 242 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- You speak on the virtue of charity, compelling the target to give away its possessions. The target must attempt a Will save. If the target has no items on its person, the spell fails. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) as it wrestles with the urge.
**Failure** On its next turn, before it does anything else, the target must present the nearest creature with an item in its possession; the target chooses which item to give, and if the only item it has is one that it's currently using to defend itself, such as a weapon during a combat encounter, it can choose to be stunned for 1 round instead of giving up the item. This might require the target to Interact to retrieve an item or move to reach the nearest creature, and passing the item off requires an Interact action as normal.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the duration is 4 rounds, and the target must repeat the effects of a failure on each of its turns. At the end of each of its turns, the target can attempt a new Will save to reduce the remaining duration by 1 round, ending the effects entirely on a critical success.
name: Charitable Urge

You deal 2d4 cold damage to creatures in the area; they must each attempt a Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.

The target must attempt a Fortitude save; creatures with the [gnome](/Traits.aspx?ID=617) trait take a –2 circumstance penalty to this save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) for 1 round.

For 1 round, if the target tries to use a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action, it must succeed at a Will save against your spell DC or the action is lost; this effect has the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) and [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) traits.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is permanently enfeebled 2 and drained 2 (although magic such as [_sound body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1679) can remove these conditions).

If the target of containment is unwilling, the effects depend on the target's Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target escapes from the field as it's forming, causing it to collapse.

<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Standard">
[Corrosive Muck](/Spells.aspx?ID=1982)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=524" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 243

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** all squares in two 10-foot bursts

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create two puddles of acidic slime that hinder the movement of anyone who walks through them. The area of each pool becomes [greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). Each round that a creature starts its turn in one of the pools or enters a pool during a move action it's using, it takes 8d6 acid damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that critically fails its saving throw also takes 1d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). As normal, if a Large or larger creature starts its turn in both pools, it takes the damage only once.

---
**Heightened (+2)** Create an additional 10-foot burst.
name: Corrosive Muck

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 243 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deities** [Otilaz](/Deities.aspx?ID=718), [Shawnari](/Deities.aspx?ID=600) **Range** touch **Target** 1 Large or smaller object, construct , or living creature **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** varies --- You curse the target with rapid aging or erosion. The effect depends on whether the target is an object, a [construct](/Traits.aspx?ID=563), or a living creature. [Artifact](/Traits.aspx?ID=537), along with objects and constructs made of precious materials (as determined by the GM), are immune. - **Object** If the object is attended, its bearer can attempt a Fortitude save. If the bearer fails or the object is unattended, the object immediately takes 4d6 damage (applying Hardness normally), and the item is cursed with an unlimited duration. Until the curse ends, the item becomes [shoddy](/Rules.aspx?ID=197) and can't be [Repaired](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384), and the curse attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any spell that would restore the object's Hit Points. [_Cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) can target an item affected by this spell. - **Construct** The construct takes 4d6 damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). On a failure, for 1 hour, the construct is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), is [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), and can't be Repaired, and the curse attempts to counteract any spell that would restore the construct's Hit Points. On a critical failure, these effects have an unlimited duration. - **Living Creature** The living creature must attempt a Fortitude save. Ageless creatures are immune. **Critical Success** The living creature is unaffected.
**Success** The living creature briefly ages, becoming [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) for 1 round.
**Failure** As success, with a duration of 1 hour.
**Critical Failure** As success, with an unlimited duration. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Curse of Lost Time

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Déjà Vu](/Spells.aspx?ID=1986)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Incapacitation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=631" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 244

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deities**
[Bergelmir](/Deities.aspx?ID=491), [Brixori](/Deities.aspx?ID=715), [Yhidothrus](/Deities.aspx?ID=441)

**Patron Theme**
[Cobyslarni](/Patrons.aspx?ID=23)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 100 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Will

**Duration** 2 rounds
</row>

</column>

---

You loop a thought process in the target's mind, forcing it to repeat a moment's worth of actions. The target must attempt a Will save. If the target fails, whatever actions the target uses on its next turn, it must repeat on its following turn. The actions must be repeated in the same order and as close to the same specifics as possible. For example, if the target makes an attack, it must repeat the attack against the same creature, if possible, and if the target moves, it must move the same distance and direction, if possible, on its next turn.

 If the target can't repeat an action, such as Casting a Spell that has been exhausted or needing to target a creature that has died, it can act as it chooses for that action but becomes [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) until the end of its turn.
name: Déjà Vu

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Dismantle](/Spells.aspx?ID=1987)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 244

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 non-magical object in your possession of 1 Bulk or less
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You touch an object, and it immediately disassembles itself into its component pieces. The spell fails if the target lacks component pieces (such as a statue carved from one block of stone), and using it on a dangerous object like a snare or trap typically triggers it. The object gains the [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60) condition, and the component pieces become small enough to be hidden under normal clothing and armor. You can Dismiss the spell. When the spell ends, the object reassembles itself into its original form, appearing in your hand or hands if you have them free, or on the ground in front of you otherwise. Once reassembled, the object loses the broken condition, and its Hit Points return to the value the object had when you Cast the Spell.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The spell lasts for 10 minutes.

**Heightened (6th)** The spell lasts until your next daily preparations.
name: Dismantle

[Dream Council](/Spells.aspx?ID=1988)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
<trait label="Sleep" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=696" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 244

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deities**
[Alazhra](/Deities.aspx?ID=510), [Naderi](/Deities.aspx?ID=627), [Nyarlathotep (The Crawling Chaos)](/Deities.aspx?ID=573)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 10 minutes

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** planetary

**Target** up to 12 creatures you know by name and have met in person
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour
</row>

</column>

---

When you Cast this Spell, any targets—including you—can choose to immediately fall asleep. The spell ends for any creatures that don't choose to fall asleep. Sleepers join a shared dream, where they can communicate with one another as though they were in the same room. Individual targets leave this shared dream upon awakening, and if all the targets awaken, the spell ends.
name: Dream Council

<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Standard">
[Drop Dead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1989)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Subtle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=522" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=727" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 244

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** A creature within range is hit by an attack from an enemy.

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

The target appears to fall down dead, though it actually turns [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83). Its illusory corpse remains where it fell, complete with a believable fatal wound. This illusion looks and feels like a dead body. If the target's death seems absurd—for instance, a barbarian at full health appears to be slain by 2 damage—the GM can grant the attacker an immediate Perception check to disbelieve the illusion. If the target uses [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), the spell ends. This ends the entire spell, so the illusory corpse disappears too.

---
**Heightened (7th)** The spell doesn't end if the target uses a hostile action.
name: Drop Dead

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Familiar’s Face](/Spells.aspx?ID=1991)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=689" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 245

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 1 mile

**Target** 1 willing creature that’s your animal companion or familiar
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained
</row>

</column>

---

The target becomes a scrying sensor, allowing you to see through its eyes, smell what it smells, and similarly use its other senses. If you Cast a Spell with the [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686) trait that affects your senses, such as [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), while this spell is active, you gain the benefit of the spell through the target's senses instead of your own. You can also speak through the target with a voice much like yours, though it takes on some of the timbre and character of the target's growls or squawks. You can use [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) on the target as part of Sustaining this spell. You don't need line of sight or line of effect to your target when you Cast this Spell.
name: Familiar’s Face

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Final Sacrifice](/Spells.aspx?ID=1992)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 245

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Target** 1 creature with the  minion  trait that you summoned or permanently control
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex

</row>

</column>

---

You channel disruptive energies through the bond between you and your minion, causing it to violently explode. The target is immediately slain, and the explosion deals 6d6 fire damage to creatures within 20 feet of it with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. If the target has the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) trait, the spell deals cold damage and has the cold trait instead of the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) trait. Attempting to cast this spell targeting a creature that you temporarily seized control of, such as an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) commanded by [_bind undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1449), automatically fails and breaks the controlling effect.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d6.
name: Final Sacrifice

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 245 **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** touch **Area** 15-foot cone **Defense** Fortitude --- Toxic spores swarm over creatures in the area, causing them to erupt in grotesque fungal growths. These noxious growths deal 2d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and each creature must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half the persistent poison damage.
**Failure** The target takes the full persistent poison damage. While it's taking this persistent poison damage, it has weakness 1 to fire and weakness 1 to slashing.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but double the persistent poison damage. While it's taking this persistent poison damage, it has weakness 2 to fire and weakness 2 to slashing. --- **Heightened (+2)** The persistent damage increases by 2d6, and the weakness increases by 1, or by 2 on a critical failure.
name: Fungal Infestation

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Ghostly Tragedy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1995)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=737" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 246

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 60-foot emanation

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

This spell compels local spirits to reenact a violent event of the recent past that you're aware of and name as you Cast the Spell. You take the role of the primary victim. The reenactment plays out the final 9 minutes leading up to the death or injury of the victim and the minute after. The spirits don't change form, so this doesn't help determine perpetrators by their looks. Spiritual forms of missing creatures necessary for the event manifest as needed, and missing items appear as shadowy outlines.

 Once the scene ends, you take 2d6 void damage for each ghostly apparition that participated in the scene (typically equal to the number of creatures involved other than the victim). Any creature that observed the ghostly recreation, including you, can attempt checks to investigate the event to discover new clues and information.
name: Ghostly Tragedy

The target must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) by its unbidden hunger.
**Failure** The target is sickened 2 and can't reduce this condition below sickened 1 until it first consumes some raw meat; if the creature doesn't have access to raw meat, it can take a bite out of a corpse within reach as an Interact action.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target can't reduce the condition below sickened 2 until it consumes raw meat.
name: Ghoulish Cravings

All creatures and unsecured objects in the area move toward the center, depending on their Reflex saving throws. This follows the rules for [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451). If there's not enough space near the center of the sphere, creatures and objects nearer to the center move first, and others move as far as they can without being blocked, up to the amount set by their saving throw outcomes. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature moves 5 feet toward the center.
**Failure** The creature moves 15 feet toward the center.
**Critical Failure** The creature moves 30 feet toward the center.
name: Gravity Well

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 246 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deity** [Sigek](/Deities.aspx?ID=729) **Range** 60 feet **Target** 1 corporeal creature **Defense** [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude --- This gruesome spell causes the target to grow excess limbs and organs, whether it be fingers multiplying until hands resemble bushes, eyes popping open in bizarre places, legs sprouting from the side of the body, or some other result. The target takes 10d6 piercing damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save) as the new features erupt. This spell has no effect on a target with a mutable anatomy or no limbs, such as an [ooze](/Traits.aspx?ID=665) or a [protean](/Traits.aspx?ID=678). The growths rot rapidly and fall away after 1 round. In addition, unless the initial target critically succeeds, creatures within 30 feet of the target, including the target, must attempt Will saves, after which they're temporarily immune to this secondary effect of _grisly growths_ for 1 hour. This additional effect is a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effect. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91).
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 2. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d6.
name: Grisly Growths

You call upon sacred energy to amplify a vial of holy water, tossing it an incredible distance. It explodes in an enormous burst that deals 3d6 bludgeoning damage to creatures in the area from the cascade of water. The water deals an additional 6d6 spirit damage to creatures with the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait in the area.

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Ice Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=2001)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=555" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 247

**Traditions**

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 20-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create a gray storm cloud that pelts creatures with an icy deluge. When you Cast the Spell, a burst of magical hail deals 2d8 bludgeoning damage and 2d8 cold damage to each creature in the area below the cloud ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Snow and sleet continue to rain down in the area for the remainder of the spell's duration, making the area [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). Any creature that ends its turn in the storm takes 2 cold damage. If you Cast this Spell outdoors, you can create two nonoverlapping clouds instead of one. As normal, if a Large or larger creature is in both clouds, it still only takes the initial damage once and the continuing damage once per turn.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The initial bludgeoning damage and cold damage increase by 1d8 each, and the cold damage creatures take at the end of their turns increases by 1.
name: Ice Storm

<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Standard">
[Imaginary Lockbox](/Spells.aspx?ID=2002)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 247

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deity**
[The Offering Plate](/Deities.aspx?ID=254)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 container and its contents, totaling 10 Bulk or less
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the next time you make your daily preparations
</row>

</column>

---

You turn a container and its contents into an imaginary form stored in your mind that only you can see and interact with. The container's physical properties—the material from which it's made, any locks, or other features—are irrelevant to the casting of this spell, but the container can't contain any creatures. The container has no Bulk, and you can visualize everything inside it.

 You can retrieve an item from the lockbox as an activity that takes 3 actions and has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) and [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) traits. Putting items back isn't possible. You can Dismiss the spell. When the spell ends, the container returns to its normal state, either appearing in your hands if it can fit there or on the ground adjacent to you if not.
name: Imaginary Lockbox

[Imprint Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=2003)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 247, [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 74

**Tradition**
[Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 object
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You project psychic vibrations onto the target object, imprinting it with a short message or emotional theme of your design. This imprinted sensation is revealed to a creature who casts [_object reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=705) on the target object, replacing any emotional events the item was present for. If the object is in the area of a [_retrocognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652) spell, the imprinted messages appear as major events in the timeline, but they don't interfere with any other visions.

 If the object is targeted with [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) of a higher spell rank than _imprint message_, the caster learns that the object has been magically modified. When you Cast this Spell, any prior vibrations placed on an object by previous castings of _imprint message_ fade.
name: Imprint Message

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Invisible Item](/Spells.aspx?ID=2004)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 247

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 object
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour
</row>

</column>

---

You make the object [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83). This makes it [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) to all creatures, though the creatures can attempt to find the target, making it [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) to them instead if they succeed. If the item is used as part of a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), the spell ends after that hostile action is completed. Making a weapon invisible typically doesn't give any advantage to the attack, except that an invisible thrown weapon or piece of ammunition can be used for an attack without necessarily giving information about the attacker's hiding place unless the weapon returns to the attacker.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** The duration is until the next time you make your daily preparations.

**Heightened (7th)** The duration is unlimited.
name: Invisible Item

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 247 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deities** [Lymnieris](/Deities.aspx?ID=649), [Vineshvakhi](/Deities.aspx?ID=622) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- You partially transform a foe's feet into unwieldy slabs of metal, slowing their steps. The target attempts a Fortitude saving throw. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70) and has weakness 2 to electricity until the end of your next turn. The spell can't be sustained.
**Failure** The target is encumbered and has weakness 2 to electricity.
**Critical Failure** The target is encumbered and has weakness 3 to electricity. --- **Heightened (+1)** The weakness increases by 1.
name: Leaden Steps

<title level="1" right="Spell 7" pfs="Standard">
[Lifewood Cage](/Spells.aspx?ID=2006)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 247

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 1 cube 20 feet on each side

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Reflex

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create an immobile, invisible prison of hardened wood suffused with vital energy from [the Forge of Creation](/Planes.aspx?ID=27). The cage is a 20-foot cube made of wooden branches, each a half inch thick and a half inch apart. Each creature in the area where you create the cage must attempt a Reflex save. If such a creature fails, it becomes trapped inside the cage. If it succeeds, it's [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) outside the cage into a space of its choice. If a creature in the area is too big to fit inside the prison, the spell automatically fails.

 The cage has AC 10, Hardness 20, and 40 Hit Points, and it's immune to critical hits and precision damage, though it has weakness 5 to void damage. A creature capable of passing through the space between the bars (typically a Tiny creature) can leave; all others are confined within. The vitality energy suffusing the wood prevents [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures from passing through the beams. Attacks with a weapon too large to fit between the bars can't pass through the cage, and the bars provide [standard cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) even against attacks that can pass through the gaps. Spells and most area effects (such as [_dragon breath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=499)) can pass through the cage uninhibited.
name: Lifewood Cage

[Lightning Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=2007)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=586" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 248

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 20-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create a black, rumbling storm cloud and call down one lightning bolt within the spell's area. The bolt is a vertical line from the top of the storm cloud to the ground below, dealing 4d12 electricity damage to creatures in the line ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On subsequent rounds, the first time you Sustain the spell each round, you can call another lightning bolt within the area. If you Cast this Spell outdoors, you can create two nonoverlapping clouds instead of one, though you can still call down only one bolt per turn.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage of each bolt increases by 1d12.
name: Lightning Storm

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 248 **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deity** [Yuelral](/Deities.aspx?ID=463) **Range** 120 feet **Area** 500-foot burst **Target** up to 5 creatures **Duration** 10 minutes --- Animals, plants, and fungi in the area turn against the targets. Each target suffers from the following effects as long as it remains in the area.
  • Vegetation springs up from every surface, giving each target a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed any time it's adjacent to the plants and fungi.
  • Aggressive animals attack unpredictably. At the start of its turn, each target rolls a DC 8 flat check. On a failure, it's attacked by creatures that deal 2d10 slashing damage. The target attempts a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save and is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round on any outcome other than a critical success.
  • The target loses any connection to nature or natural creatures. The target must succeed at a DC 5 flat check when casting any [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) spell or the spell fails. Furthermore, [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531), [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614), and [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) creatures become [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) to it, even one with a strong bond to the target, such as an animal companion.
The GM might decide that you can't subject some creatures, such as an emissary of a nature deity, to the ire of nature.
name: Nature’s Enmity

You emit a cloud of toxic smoke that temporarily obscures you from sight. Each creature except you in the area when you Cast the Spell takes 1d6 poison damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). A creature that critically fails the saving throw also becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91). All creatures in the area become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and all creatures outside the smoke become concealed to creatures within it. This smoke can be dispersed by a strong wind.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6.

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Object Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=2012)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 248, [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 74

**Tradition**
[Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deities**
[Aerekostes](/Deities.aspx?ID=699), [Gaasham](/Deities.aspx?ID=716)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 object
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You place a hand on an object to learn a piece of information about an emotional event that occurred involving the object within the past week, determined by the GM. If you cast object reading on the same item multiple times, you can either concentrate on a single event to gain additional pieces of information about that event or gain a piece of information about another emotional event in the applicable time frame.

---
**Heightened (2nd)** You can learn about an event that occurred within the last month.

**Heightened (4th)** You can learn about an event that occurred within the last year.

**Heightened (6th)** You can learn about an event that occurred within the last decade.

**Heightened (8th)** You can learn about an event that occurred within the last century.

**Heightened (9th)** You can learn about an event that occurred within the entirety of the object's history.
name: Object Reading

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 249 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 60 feet **Target** 1 living creature **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- A phantasmal image of the most precious thing imaginable to the target appears in a location of your choice within the spell's range. Only the spell's target can see the treasure, though you can see the vague shape of the treasure—be it a pile of items, a deific avatar, or a cherished loved one or hero. The target's response to the treasure is based on the outcome of the target's Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with the treasure, and the duration is until the end of its turn. The target can also try to disbelieve the illusion if it touches the treasure, [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) to examine it, or speaks to it if the illusion appears to be a person or the like. If the target disbelieves the illusion, the spell ends.
**Failure** As success, but the duration is 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the duration is 1 minute. The target finds the treasure so appealing that until the spell ends, it must spend each action focused on it. This can include moving toward the treasure if the target isn't next to it and Interacting with the treasure if the target is next to it. (If the illusion appears to be a person or the like, the target can also Interact to converse with it.)
name: Phantasmal Treasure

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 249 **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 30 feet **Target** you and up to 5 willing targets **Duration** 1 minute --- Summoning the power of the natural world, you transform the targets into a herd of mammoths, and they each assume a Huge battle form. Each target must have enough space to expand into or the spell fails for that target. Each target gains the animal trait. Each target can Dismiss the spell's effects on itself. Each target gains the following while transformed.
  • AC = 22 + the target's level. Ignore any armor check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 20 temporary Hit Points.
  • Speed 40 feet.
  • [Low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416).
  • The following unarmed melee attacks, which are the only attacks the target can use to Strike. When Striking with these attacks, the target uses their attack modifier with the proficiency and item bonuses of their most favorable weapon or unarmed Strike, and the damage is listed for each attack. These attacks are Strength based (for the purpose of the enfeebled condition, for example).
    **Melee** tusk ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), Damage 4d8+19 piercing; **Melee** trunk ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), Damage 4d6+16 bludgeoning; **Melee** foot ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), Damage 4d6+13 bludgeoning.
  • Athletics modifier of +30, unless the target's own modifier is higher.
  • Each target can use the Trample action.
  • **Trample** You move up to twice your Speed and move through the space of Large or smaller creatures, trampling each creature whose space you enter. A trampled creature takes damage from your foot Strike based on a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save (DC = 19 + your level).
name: Primal Herd

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Protector Tree](/Spells.aspx?ID=2015)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=668" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 249

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Deities**
[Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), [Jaidi](/Deities.aspx?ID=406), [Uirch](/Deities.aspx?ID=560), [Verilorn](/Deities.aspx?ID=696)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

A Medium tree suddenly grows in an unoccupied square within range. The tree has AC 10 and 10 Hit Points. Whenever an ally adjacent to the tree is hit by a Strike, the tree interposes its branches and takes the damage first. Any additional damage beyond what it takes to reduce the tree to 0 Hit Points is dealt to the original target. The tree isn't large enough to impede movement through its square.

 If the tree is in soil and survives to the end of the spell's duration, it remains as an ordinary, non-magical tree and continues to grow and thrive. The GM might determine that the tree disappears immediately in certain inhospitable situations.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The tree has an additional 10 Hit Points.
name: Protector Tree

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Reaper’s Lantern](/Spells.aspx?ID=2017)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=542" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Death" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=571" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=640" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 249

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 5-foot emanation

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Fortitude

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You call forth a ghostly lantern that guides the living toward death and the undead toward true death. It sheds bright light in the spell's area and dim light to twice that area. The lantern is insubstantial and floats near you, suspended from an ephemeral, skeletal hand. Living creatures and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) in the area when you Cast the Spell, or that enter the area later, must attempt Fortitude saves. Living creatures that fail their Fortitude saves gain only half the normal benefit from [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) effects while within the area. Undead targets that fail their Fortitude saves become [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) while within the area. Once a creature attempts a save against reaper's lantern, it uses the same outcome if it leaves the area and enters it again.

 Once per turn, starting on the round after you cast _reaper's lantern_, you can Sustain the spell to increase the emanation's radius by 5 feet. When you do so, you force creatures in the area that haven't yet attempted a save against _reaper's lantern_ to attempt one.
name: Reaper’s Lantern

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 250 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Deities** [Folgrit](/Deities.aspx?ID=448), [Gaasham](/Deities.aspx?ID=716), [The Deliberate Journey](/Deities.aspx?ID=250) **Cast** 1 minute **Range** 500 feet **Duration** until your next daily preparations --- You conjure a towering mansion up to four stories tall and up to 300 feet on a side. While Casting the Spell, you hold an image of the mansion and its desired appearance in your mind. The mansion can contain as many or as few rooms as you desire, and it's decorated as you imagine it. You can imagine a purpose for each room of the mansion, and the proper accouterments appear within. Any furniture or other mundane fixtures function normally for anyone inside the mansion, but they cease to exist if taken beyond its walls. No fixture created with this spell can create magical effects, but magical devices brought into the mansion function normally.
Your mansion contains the same types and quantities of foodstuffs and servants as created by the [_planar palace_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1634) spell. Each of the mansion's exterior doorways and windows are protected by [_alarm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1439) spells. You choose whether each alarm is audible or mental as you Cast the Spell, and each has a different sound (for an audible alarm) or sensation (for a mental one), allowing you to instantly determine which portal has been used.
name: Resplendent Mansion

<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
</traits>

[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Area** 10-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Reflex

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Misshapen skeletal forms erupt from a solid surface, such as a stone floor, and fill the burst. The area they fill is difficult terrain. Their grasping claws deal 2d6 slashing damage to creatures on the ground in the area when the skeletons first appear with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

 On subsequent rounds, the first time you Sustain the Spell each round, you can move the area of skeletons up to 20 feet within the range of the spell and deal 2d6 slashing damage with a basic Reflex save to each creature in the new area.

 Damaging or destroying the skeletons is irrelevant, as new bones pull forth from the ground to repair and replace any that are obliterated.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Rouse Skeletons
